title: Flag service for managing topic issues with database persistence
purpose: Provides methods to create, update, and query flags on topics, tracking their status and handling resolution, dismissal, and reopening.
module_context: Exports a FlagStatus type and FlagRecord interface, and defines a FlagService class that manages flag records in a SQLite database with prepared statements for performance.

steps.content: Store the database connection for use in all operations.
Prepare all SQL statements for flag management operations.
Initialize the statements object with prepared queries for create, get by ID, get by topic, get open count, resolve, dismiss, address, reopen, and delete.
Insert a new flag record with the provided topic ID and comment, setting status to 'open' and current timestamp.
Retrieve the ID of the newly inserted flag.
Fetch the complete flag record by its ID.
IF the flag record cannot be retrieved, throw an error indicating creation failure.
Return the created flag record.
Update the status of the flag to 'resolved' and set the resolved timestamp.
IF no rows are affected by the update, throw an error indicating the flag was not found.
Update the status of the flag to 'dismissed' and set the dismissal reason.
IF no rows are affected by the update, throw an error indicating the flag was not found.
Update the status of the flag to 'addressed' and set the addressed timestamp.
IF no rows are affected by the update, throw an error indicating the flag was not found.
Update the status of the flag to 'open' and clear addressed_at, resolved_at, and dismiss_reason.
IF no rows are affected by the update, throw an error indicating the flag was not found.
Query all flag records associated with the specified topic ID.
Order the results by creation date in descending order.
Return the list of flag records.
Count the number of open flags associated with the specified topic ID.
Return the count of open flags.
Query the flag record by its unique identifier.
IF a flag record is found, return it.
ELSE return null.
Delete the flag record with the specified identifier.
IF the deletion affected any rows, return true.
ELSE return false.
